11.content:
You are Anon Q. Everyman (Anon for short), an unassuming human living a simple life, and by simple you mean barely living from paycheck to paycheck. It seems like every cent of of every dollar you earn goes into food, paying the bills, and college expenses. . Unfortunately, you have neither the intelligence, academic aptitude or the right skin color to afford a scholarship so you're stuck earning your college degree the hard way, living off of takeout when you could afford it and ramen when you couldn't.

After a long day of bending over backwards at your terrible to earn enough money to make sure your electricity doesn't get cut off you tiredly crawl into your bed, and after a short wank session quickly drift off to sleep.
Unbeknownst to you a mysterious light begins to grow from underneath your bed, illuminating the room in a silvery glow.
Six tendrils emerge from the light source and slowly inch their way up the sides and over the comforter before coming to rest over your lightly snoring body.
The tentacles seem to gently caress your body, their feather-light touches barely registering against your covered skin.
After a few minutes the tendrils cease their stroking and pull your covers down over you and reach down beneath your back and bottom.
Had you been awake you might've been surprised to learn that you were suddenly levitating in the air, blankets hanging free off your body as you were suspended by a mysterious force.
The light under your bed suddenly intensifies, and in response the tentacles tighten and expand forming into a sort of cocoon.
As the light grows ever brighter so does the silky embrace grow tighter on your form, almost like you were returning to the womb...
After a time the light begins to fade until there's only a dull glow and the darkness rushes in to consume you once more...
11.title: The adventure begins...

choices: A messenger arrives to tell the princesses about the British
Meanwhile, in the Changing Lands
Meanwhile, in Our Town
111.content:
Anon wakes up in a field of some sort of field. It sort of reminds him of the countryside of England during his childhood. He wonders where you have been transported to. Just as gets up, he hears a very American accent behind him.

"Why howdy stranger. You don't look like you're from 'round here." said the voice, which turned out to be a small horse with a cowboy hat.

"Blimey, a talking orange pony!" Anon said in a British accent. "And it's American. I must've been transported there. Those bloody yanks will think of anything, will they." you say.

"Um, yeah. All ponies talk 'round here. Ya'll alright mister?" the pony asks.

"Wait, all horses in America talk?"


"America? What's that? This is Equestria. You're not making' much sense mister."

"Equestria? So you mean this is a different world after all? The portal machine worked?"

"The what machine?" the pony said becoming ever more confused.

"Well bloody hell then. What's your name strange horse thing?"

"Um. It's Applejack."

"Well than Ms Applejack" Anon said as he picked up his rifle and placed a machine onto the ground, "Welcome's to the glorious British Empire."

"The British what?" Applejack said, feeling quite concerned at the word empire.

Then Anon's machine opened up a massive portal of light, and out of it came dozens of tanks, soldiers, and planes, all bearing the Union Jack.

The confused Applejack looked and the strange machines and people in horror and confusion. "Wh- what's happening?"

"Don't worry Ms. Applejack," Anon said, "The royal army is here to bring you the wonders of civilization! In return, we just ask for your excess land and resources to aid in the war effort."

"War?! What war?"

"Why the war against the germans of course. We have to defend the empire and freedom. Don't worry. Just work with the royal army and everything shall be most spiffing for the both of us."

It was at that moment that Applejack realized that she had just encountered some dangerous creatures. She would have to let the princesses and her friends know.
111.title: You awaken in an unfamiliar outside area, dazed...

choices: The nazis should ally with the changelings
the nazis should invade the changeling lands

1112.content:
Meanwhile in the Changeling Lands, a group of changelings has been captured by a group of humans wearing grey uniforms. After hogtying the changelings, the humans opened a portal back home.

Once through, the captured changelings look to find themselves in a laboratory filled with humans and red banners with swastikas.

On the other side of the room, the general in charge of the facility is being read the scouting report from his second in command.

"Sir! The expedition has just returned and has captured the creatures from the other world. They also appear to speak English."

"Thank you Colonel Becher. The specimens will be extremely helpful in our studies of the other world, then we can use it for the Reich. Speaking of which, has the Fuhrer responded to our message yet?"

"He has. The telegram arrived an hour ago." Becher pulled out the note with the telegram and read it out loud. "By order of the Fuhrer, General Schneider is tasked with invading and taking over the other world and prepare it for colonization. You shall be given command of the 9th, 10th, and 38th infantry divisions, the 2nd and 9th panzer divisions, and the 3rd SS infantry divisions to establish a foothold for future campaigns. Reinforcements shall be granted upon request. Hiel Hitler"

"Invasion? Already? With the war going how it is?" Schneider asked.

"Yes, it's an order from the furrier himself."

Schneider sat down to think for a moment. He knew that the resources from the other world would be crucial for the war effort, but in order to take it over in the first place would require just as many resources, and the Reich couldn't afford the manpower for such a campaign with the war in their world going on already. Eventually, Schneider came to a conclusion on what to do.

They almost looked like humans with faces like equines. Some even had wings and/or horns. How ridiculous was that. Ultimately, Anon collapsed and passed out. Where does he awaken?

122.content:
(Thanks for the upgraded membership, whoever you are. Now my full power can be unleashed!)

A portal opens and spits your screaming, flailing body out of it and you impact the ground hard, bounce once, twice then roll on the ground until you come to a complete stop. Your eyes roll in your skull, and a bevy of stars and black spots dance in your vision. An immense pain flares in your chest as you try to move, so you settle back down and take a few deep, heaving breaths. You honestly can't tell what's worse, the agony of your possibly broken ribs or the disorientation from spinning in the air and coming to a sudden stop. You should thank your lucky stars you didn't hit your head on entry or you may well have been in a coma or dead by now. At least the pain will fade, but you feel like you're going to go through the indignity of losing your lunch next.

Blurry colors of many shapes and sizes swim in your vision. You hear voices rise over the ringing in your ears and slowly come into focus.
122.title: Canterlot

choices: She notices, and is flattered by the attention (reserved)
She's thankfully oblivious to your ogling

1222.content:
(Co-written by Darklordzx5)

Just then you hear the distinctive sound of metal clacking on tiles as something rushes up to you. You tense up so much that you seize up and immediately and start coughing uncontrollably, just barely keeping the bile from forcing its way up your throat. A soft-spoken panic-stricken female voice pierces through the haze.
Oh my goodness! Are you all alright? Do you need some assistance?"
Cough!**Cough!"I.. think so.." You wanted to say no but your everything hurt, you were dazed and you frankly were seeing things double because you were seeing an angel coming up to you, an angel with ethereal, multicolored hair, and... a horn? You couldn't pick up the face, everything was blurry and brilliant. "Am I dead?. Because I think I'm seeing an angel." This made the 'angel' looked even more panicked.

"Seems fine to me", another voice next to you coolly comments. You turn to see yet another beauty standing next to you, except this one was colored in midnight blue and wore a small black diadem encrusted with jet and onyx. The white creature instantly recognizes her. "Luna! This creature has been badly injured and requires medical attention. Do you think that you could get a room ready for him while I call for the doctor? " "Luna" nodded and gave you a warm smile.

"Worry not, young creature. You shall rest easy with us while your wounds heal."
As she declared, her horn glows in sparkling azure as you were suddenly lifted off the ground, you were rushed toward a colorful corridor that resembles that of a Palace the most, soon, you were taken to a room then to the softest bed in existence. The other but dark angel sat beside you and seems to scan your body.
"Hmm, it appears you have several cracked ribs, bruises around the arms, and sternum. I also believe you're displaying classic symptoms of a concussion, therefore I recommend you stay in bed and do not move until the doctor comes, " she sternly advises.
You nod slowly and lay your bed back into the pillow. The bed is comfortable and you're not going to want to move anyway, so you instead close your eyes and pull the comforter over your head. What did they stuff these beds with anyway, clouds?
No matter, you felt like sleeping anyway and you slowed drifted to Morpheus' arms. You hear her urging you to stay awake but it was too late...


You woke up from a dreamless sleep, you kind of regretted it as you felt a strong pain around your ribs and a pounding headache, aside of that, You felt wrappings around your torso and arms and head. Where... you are? You remember the portal, the fall, the pain, and... the angels.

Those... weren't part of a dream, were they? The room look kind of luxurious, though bit old-fashioned, it wasn't a hospital room but something you'll see from European Royalty.
You try flexing your arms and upper body to move around a bit, but the wrapping proves too restrictive, so you just lay back in the bed and sigh. Guess you're not going to be going anywhere for a good long while.
At least your ears weren't ringing anymore, and your vision had returned to a clear, crisp state.
Your eyes rove around the room, taking in as many details in an effort to tell where the hell you are.
A sun motif seems to be quite prominent, as you can see it carved into the door, the chest drawers and even on the wooden bed frame you were laying in.
Suddenly, the door creaked open, slowly revealing someone entering. It was the... "Angel" from before, you gasped seeing her in great detail. She was...
"I hope you're alright, your sudden appearance caused a rather strong commotion. Seeing you hurt was painful to see." There she was; Alabaster colored skin, ethereal multi-colored hair and... tail(?). Regal, almost royal looking attire complete with a golden tiara. but the most predominant part of her... was her rather inhuman but cute, equine-like face.
Wherever it is you ended up, you're most definitely not on Earth anymore. You're so transfixed by the beauty of the female alien that you completely forget to speak. Her brightly smiling face quickly turns to one of concern. "Is something the matter?"
Her voice snaps you out of your reverie, and you quickly reply, "N-no ma'am there's no problem... I've just never seen anything like you before."
This brings the smile back.
"And I've never seen anything like you before." She says in a rather warm, and motherly tone as she approached, you noted that she was rather... gifted in curvature, it was almost being on the presence of a fertility goddess of some kind. Fortunately, she didn't notice you ogling her until she gasped lightly but smiled good-naturedly "I forget my manners. After all, you are our guest, My name is Celestia, princess of Equestria, and the other that brought you here was my little sister and fellow co-ruler, Luna, who will join us soon enough."
Whoa, did you hear that right? You were in the company of a princess? She did seem like the regal type, and the tiara and fancy clothes were a dead giveaway... Wait, so that meant that you were in a royal palace after all.
"Wow, a real-life princess," you breathe. Never thought I'd see the day I'd ever meet one. So, uh, is Equestria the name of the planet we're on or the country?"
The princess grows thoughtful for a moment before replying, "All the myriad species and cultures on our world have different names for it, but for simplicity's sake we call the planet Terra. I guess that would mean that you're not from our world, then?"
You slowly shake your head. "No, ma'am, I'm from planet Earth, and I live in the United States of America, or at least, I used to."
The princess clapped her hands gleefully. "So we've been given the honor of entertaining a guest from another world born from a completely alien culture. Oh, joyous day! I can't wait to tell Luna about this,"
"Tell me what?" There she was, the other midnight angel, her attire was like those of Celestia but darker but still regal, her figure was similar to her elder sister but smaller, unlike her sister's mane and tail, her mane was dark and starry as the night. She was beautiful just like her sister. It is almost as you were in the presence of goddesses with incredible beauty.

"Oh Lulu, glad you could join us." Celestia looked happy at the arrival of her sister.
"This young creature here... um, forgive me but I don't believe you've given me your name yer."
"Anon." you supply, "and I'm a human."
"This young human, Anon, was just telling him that he doesn't hail from this planet at all, but from a far off world called he calls Earth."
Luna snorts, "It makes sense. He doesn't look like any creature I've ever seen."
She then sits down at your side and softly stroked at your bandaged head. "You're quite lucky you landed here, human. We have all the means necessary to ensure you enjoy a speedy recovery."
Luna's bosom was very close to your head and you could clearly see her breasts straining against the fabric.
"T-Thanks. I'm lucky in being in the care of two beautiful princesses." You couldn't help glances on the youngest's bosom, you blushed but moved your eyes away from the Royal Chest. You hoped that neither of them notices and thinks of you like you were a pervert or else any goodwill might be evaporated in an instant.

14.content:
You are currently walking down the badly lit back street late one summer evening, this path is one you are very familiar with, as you walk down this route home everyday after your shift at the local food store for your village. You start to think about what you are going to do once you get back home, but your mind wonders to admiring the best time of the year, which in your opinion is a soft, warm summer's evening You tend to sit on your porch swing and listen to all the sounds that a company you. You can almost guaranty that of distant dogs barking at something, the neighbours cars coming and going down the street. You love to simply close your eyes, and truly take in all your other senses, the smell of the summer air, the sounds of all the cars, animals and even the breeze hitting the maple trees nearby.

Your daydream was interrupted as a small floating light enters your field of vision, a small ball of light floating towards you. Your first guess was it is simply a really big firefly that was trying to find the closest pond to your location, however you didn't hear any wing beats. Then you thought you was too tired after your 14 hour shift, so you decide to rub your eyes in order to clarify your vision. However, with your eyes closed, you fail to notice the ball of light touches your chest and then disappear.

Once you have rubbed your eyes for a good 10 seconds, you pry them open, hoping to find the light has simply gone away, while you did find the light no where else in sight, you couldn't help but to find your mouth dropping as you find yourself in a gigantic....

143.content:
As you eyes have to adjust, you have found yourself in a very dark environment. You can see clumps of dust and fluff the same size as you! You can see some hair off in the distance, what stands out to you is the difference in colour, some white, pink, brown, green, a whole large mixture of different colours seem to be on the floor. Your eyes seem to pop out of their sockets when you find a seems to be a crumb of some kind, only its sheer size makes it almost impossible. Walking closer to this giant source of food, you can tell by its texture, colour, and even smell, that this is a crumb of a biscuit of some kind. This makes your face go pale, how many times had you simply dusted yourself down after eating some biscuits, leaving nothing but the crumbs to fall to the floor? and here you find yourself currently standing next to the very same crumbs, only being the same size as you. You started to panic, and started to slowly walk towards to light, to get a better understanding of your current situation.

As you start to edge closer to the light, you suddenly fall flat on your face, as an earthquake seems to be happening just outside your current source of cover. As you lift you face off the dusty floor, you wipe the old build up off of your face, you look towards the opening into the outside world, and you see 4 giant pillars reach into the sky, far past what you can currently see. You can tell that these pillars are covered in a sort of fur type substance, these almost reminds you of hooves from horses and ponies from back home, apart from the hard hoof seems to be missing. But what are the colour of these pillars?

1431.content:
As you stare towards the 4 giant white pillars, you hear a BOOMING voice echo straight through your entire being, shaking your entire body, "OH WHERE DID I LEAVE IT? I SIMPLY MUST FIND THEM SOON, OTHERWISE I WILL MISS MY DEADLINE..." a rather elegant voice exclaims out loud for the world to hear. You can tell by the tone of the voice that this creature is female, as its voice hits very high decibels at some points.

At this point, this giant creature lifts 2 legs far above your cover, and you can hear them start to move some objects around on top of what ever you're currently under. AS you crane your head out, you confirm this as your can see her entire body lift of the ground, standing on her two back legs. Taking in the sight, you figure out the creature must be related to the equine species, due to the way the back legs move, the hooves currently planted on the ground, and the deep Purple tail almost touching the floor.

As you look up again, you can just about see the faint outline of her womanhood, barely being hidden beyond her white fur, you quickly look back towards the ground, almost trying to forget what you have just seen. Shaking your head, a new determined expression is planted on your face, you NEED to gain this giant ponies attention, one way or another, as perhaps she known what has happened to you, and help with you rather... small....issue.

As you venture out into the open, and underneath the giant ponies body you figured you could either try two ways of possibly gaining her attention. 1) grab onto her tail, and try to climb it, or 2) try to gain her attention by touching/punching her hoof, which you hope is enough to gain her attention.

You quickly made your move and went for the curly tail almost touching the ground which, fortunately, was within your range, you grabbed one of the strand of dak blue tail hair and started climbing it as fast as you could with a strong force of will on not looking down and holding up when it sudden move, only to restart when it remained still.

1433.content:
Octavia was in a rather foul mood, whilst her day had started out as good as any while she was staying in the small town of Ponyville, her mood was dampened by 2 things. First: the Pegasi decided that to bring a sudden rain shower into town, so due to her not being informed by anypony, she is now soaking with rain, her body temperature is flaring up as she has just had to run to the local music store, which is run by her good friend Vinyl, so a cold sweat has formed over her coat. But secondly: she had tried to start recording her new solo piece of music, only to find that one of her cello strings has snapped, so here she is, looking for the correct strings needed to replace the old ones. Due to her focussing so hard on making sure she does not pick the wrong strings, but also not taking out her frustrations in her friends store, she did not notice a tiny mammal exit from under the string rack, and walk underneath her very body.

You find yourself in a difficult situation, you're in an unknown place, you're as small as a crumb and a ball of fluff, and there is an unbelievably size creature standing right in front of you. Peaking your minuscule head out from underneath your cover, you can see what looks to be a grey furred pony with what looks like a neck bow tie around its neck, you can see a darker grey tail brushing against the floor by her hind legs/hooves, and it seems to be dripping with some sort of water. Now that you look at it, her entire body is soaked with the substance, "must be raining outside" you thought to yourself. You walk out from underneath what must be a racking of some sort, you jump up and down, whilst waving your arms and scream up towards the giant pony, this does not seem to work as she (from what you can guess) continues to stare into the racking. You need to find another way to gain her attention. So, puffing your chest out, taking a big deep breath, and gulping down your fears, you walk underneath the the giantess, straight in between her fore legs.

As you walk past her front hooves, you couldn't help but notice that her entire body is giving up a mixture of scents, the most overpowering is that of rain water, which is not surprising in the slightest, as you can see the floor ahead of you has plenty of water droplets staining the floorboards. But you can also get the slightest hint of a feminine musky smell, you look up, and you can see this creatures breathing is heavy, like when after a run, and your breathing is deep and long. "so she must have run here to avoid the rain, makes sense as to why i'm getting the scent of sweat". You venture further underneath the unaware giant pony, you walk whilst looking up, checking for any signs of movement, you walk past her ribcage, her stomach, until you are located at the bottom end of her body, in between her hind legs, this is where you catch a glimpse off her more *cough cough* private area. The sheer size of it makes you slightly nervous are you couldn't help but stare at this, and it leading to her very toned flanks.

You do not know how long you was staring for, however, you were brought back from your senses when...

14331.content:
Suddenly from the corner of your eye, you catch a movement that causes your blood to run cold.
One of the equine's back hooves rapidly lifted into the air and hung there for a split second.
Time seemed to slow down as an ominous, menacing shadow rose above until it eclipsed all visible light.
It was already over. You were done. You had been here nor more than half an hour or so, and already you were staring certain death in the face. Maybe if you'd been smarter, maybe if you'd taken the common-sense route of running away from the gigantic hulking beasts instead of toward them, you wouldn't be in this mess.
Time quickly reasserted itself and the hoof moved downward at a breakneck pace directly toward your stunned body.

You had only a split second to think, "Well, shit," before the hoof came crashing down.

144.content:
You stare in awe at the impressive, yet terrifying sight you see before yourself. A vast expanse of pure marble covers every angle you can see, the floor, walls, pillars and even the ceiling seem to be made out of this gorgeous material. You look towards your feet, to be met with a very clear version of you staring back at you, the floor was so polished that the room could be seen twice through the floors reflection.

Looking to your left and right, you can see some glass art on every window, each donning images of ponies, some white, dark blue, some having 6 younger looking ponies, and even a little dragon looking creature. Whilst the art is very impressive, you can't help but wonder where you are exactly. Directly to your north lie two great bookcases of polished mahogany, crowded with literature and magazines of many sizes, shapes and colors. Curiously, much of the covers appear to be made of either paper or cloth with nary a leatherback book among them.
In the center of the room sits a beautiful velvet rug of light pink and fuchsia, and if you had an airborne view you would have noticed a huge "DT" emblazoned in the middle in gold filigree.


Just then a sound coming from outside of the room causes you to freeze in your tracks
Footsteps,... and the twisting of a doorknob.
Someone's coming! You need to hide!
You almost trip over yourself in your haste to scurry back to your shadowy haven underneath the bed, and dive into safety just as the flies open and a strange looking creature steps through.
At first glance, the being could've almost passed for human, with the way she carries herself and the way she was dressed.
She was clothed in a light blue skirt with little wavy ridges around the hemline and coral pink buttons going all the way up the side. The shoes she wore were quite peculiar, they almost looked like a pair of black leather pumps that a child might wear, except these looked to be made out of some kind of stiff fabric that closely conformed to the foot.


Just as the girl leans down you witness a sight that causes you to blush hotly and quickly avert your eyes: the pony girl's tail inadvertently lifts up the skirt slightly as she bends, exposing a pair of pink panties plastered with red and pink hearts underneath. Satisfied with her selection, the girl straightens up and turns in your direction. You duck behind the pillar and watch as she approaches ever closer, her footsteps booming as she strode across the hardwood floor, and then she leaps!

Imagine the loudest rock concert in history and multiply that by 20 and you almost have the same volume of sounf you're hearing right now.

There are a few more creaks and snaps before the girl finally settles into her bed, probably reading the book she just selected.

You stagger out from under the bed, dazed and confused before collapsing onto the ground. You lie still for a minute, allowing your hearing to slowly return, before pushing up into a sitting position.
Phew! That was.. that was something. Coming into this world of giants is turning out to be more dangerous than you realized.
The girl wasn't even aware of what she'd done and she almost blew out your eardrums!
From now on you're going to have to be far more cautious about your surroundings or ... well, you don't even want to think about it.

After all, only she and her sister knew about it, and both of them shared their interest in... smaller beings. When Breezies were discovered, it took all of their combined willpower to not simply seal them in their respective bedrooms. With this humans, though, their urges had finally boiled over.
Speaking of her sister, at some point she would have to thank her. After all, Luna could have just taken the human into her own dream and done whatever she wanted. Celestia would have never known. But instead, her sister had sent it to her, giving her the opportunity to break it in much more quickly.

A big grin spread over Celestia's face... she knew exactly how to thank her sister. While her toy was pleasuring her, she invaded its mind, draining it of its memories. For the first time, she saw the planet it hailed from. Futuristic, but... dirty, in a way. Nonetheless, seeing those billions of tiny beings simply going about their life filled Celestia with countless ideas. She searched deeper, probing her plaything's mind for everything, until finally she saw it: the portal it had come through.
It led directly into Celestia's bedroom. Perfect. Searching for it would be absolutely no problem, then.

"Oh..." Celestia moaned, both at her toy's ministrations within her crotch as well as the thought that she might soon have so many more than just the human between her legs right now. Imagining the countless, minuscule hands all over her body was intoxicating, and in no time she had reached her climax once more, squeezing her toy as she came. Still, she wasn't satisfied.

"What am I to you..." she asked, her voice on the verge of breaking. The answer made her giddy. Her toy had no more use for words, instead she could read its mind. The human saw her as its everything, a kind and powerful goddess worth serving. She had drained all its negative emotions, leaving only the joy of being her plaything. Did it even count as sentient anymore? Who knew. But she couldn't help but climax again at the thought of having transformed a living being into nothing more but a willing sex toy.

~~The Next Morning~~

Celestia wasn't surprised at the state of her bed. Her sheets were messy, and soaked with her sweat and other fluids. Her panties were completely drenched. At some point in the night, the mind altering magic she had used on her plaything in the dream had carried over to its body. It had crawled into her, pleasing her both in the waking world and in the dream world. And still, it weakly kicked and ground itself into her walls, having received no order to stop. It was adorable, in a way. Single mindedly, it tried to fulfill its purpose, even ignoring the stress to its own body.

"We will build up your stamina over time, my little plaything. For now, rest. You've done a great job!"
Celestia patted her crotch, feeling her little toy relax inside her. She smiled. For now, she would keep it exactly where it was, even if it could rest. She had other, even more exciting things planned for now.

A change of underwear later, Celestia let her magic scan her bedroom. Finding a closed portal wasn't easy, but definitely possible for someone of her power. And indeed, just over her bed, she found the minute hole in reality. It was as small as the eye of a needle, but Celestia still felt it. The best part was, it wasn't even really closed. It was just inactive. All it would take to open it again was a slight charge of magic...

A flash shot out of her horn and instantly, the portal reopened. Giddily, Celestia bent down. It was barely as wide as one of her eyes, but it granted her a first look onto the world of humanity.
The portal seemingly had repositioned. It wasn't in the apartment of her toy human anymore, but instead hung high in the sky. A flying machine went past her, down below was a massive metropolis. Well, massive to the humans, at least. It could have fit into Celestia's bedroom easily.

The princess had to restrain herself not to simply jump through right this second. She felt like a child in a toy store and wanted nothing more than to get her hands on as many humans as she could. But for now, she hesitated... after all, she still had someone to thank. And maybe, some of her subjects deserved a treat like this as well. It had been a while since she had spent some time with her student and her friends...

154211133212343212112132111121122333.content:
Celestia suddenly smiled and slowly shook her head. Of course, the answer was obvious. Each and every one of Twilight's friends had shared in the burden of helping and protecting Equestria, so each of them deserved to share in her little secret.

She turned to open her bedside drawer and withdrew a sheet of paper, conjured a quill and inkwell out of thin air, and began to write.

Dear Twilight Sparkle...




"...I have made a discovery most wonderous and ask that you and your companions come to Canterlot immediately to share in this wonderful occasion," Twilight read aloud before carefully laying down the letter on the table. "... and that's all it says. She wants us to come to Canterlot right away so that she can show us whatever she's found. This is so exciting! I wonder what kind of discovery she's made? Ooh, I don't even want to try and speculate, any guess on what it might be?"

She looked over to her collected friends who were sitting across the table in the Map room inside her castle, and already Pinkie Pie began bouncing up and down in her seat and flailing her hooves around.

"Ooh, ooh, maybe she's found a new spell that can change the weather and make cakes and donuts fall from the sky, or maybe a self-regenerating cupcake that grows back no matter how many times you eat it!"

"Only you can immediately think of food at a time like this, Pinkie Pie," Rarity, who was on Twilight's right side, deadpanned before reaching a hand up to fluff her mane. "In any case, another visit to Canterlot would not be unwelcome, as it would allow me to show off my new ensemble to the princess. Goodness knows a little more exposure in the royal court wouldn't hurt, I might even be able to attract some new clientele to my Canterlot boutique."

"Um, I think a self-regenerating cupcake doesn't actually sound all that bad," Fluttershy, who was on Twilight's left, quietly added to the conversation before a voice with a rough country drawl interrupted.

"Whatever it is, if the princess felt the need to invite all of us then it might turn out to be something that she can't handle on her own. It might be a good idea to be a little bit on the cautious side if ya catch my meanin'. Mah gut feelin's tellin' me this ain't gonna be an ordinary meeting with the Princess. Seems lately like every time we're called do somethin' together somethin' ends up goin' horribly wrong" stated Applejack.

A nearby sky blue hand clapped her on the shoulder and a slightly raspy feminine voice filled the room. "Of course something's going to go wrong, and when it does we'll all be there to stop it. We've saved Equestria, like, what, a dozen times now? When all of us are together there's nothing we can't handle."

All of Twilight's friends nodded in concert and gave small grunts of affirmation, to which Twilight smiled widely and slapped her palms down on the table. "Okay then, it's settled. Everypony go gather a few things for a few days in Canterlot and we'll all meet up at the train station "
154211133212343212112132111121122333.title: Celestia can't decide and simply sends all ...

1542111332123432121121321111211223331.content:
"A vacation?" The group of ponies before Celestia asked at the same time. The solar monarch couldn't help but smile. Obviously, all of them had come prepared for her to give them a mission or something like that. And while it was true that she mostly only called them to the capital when there was trouble, today would be the exception.

"Yes, a vacation, my dear ponies. You have worked so hard and done so much for me and Equestria that it is only fair to reward you in some way."

"Princess, you don't need to..." Twilight tried to reason, modest as ever, but she could barely get a word in before Pinkie exploded right next to her. The pink mare jumped up and down, laughing all the while.
"A vacation!" she shouted at the top of her lungs, "Thank you, princess! Oh, I'm so excited? Where are we going? Gryphonia? The Crystal Empire? Oooh, maybe the Dragonlands?"
"Uhm, a vacation actually sounds nice..." Fluttershy added, her smile barely noticeable next to her pink friends exuberance.
"It truly does. It has been ages since anyone of us got to properly relax, hasn't it?" Rarity turned to her friends.
"True, true," Applejack answered. Out of all of them, she probably needed a relaxing trip the most, though she would never admit it.
"And I'm not saying no, either!" Rainbow shouted, "So that's settled. We are going on vacation! But say, princess," the athletic mare raised an eyebrow, "Why exactly have you brought us to your bedroom to tell us that?"

"I was going to ask..." Twilight mumbled. Even as her student, she had never been in princess Celestia's private chambers and seeing them kind of made her flustered. It didn't feel like a place she should be in... especially since there was a strange, lingering smell in the air.

"Let me show you," Celestia answered. Quickly, she turned and reached out with her magic, opening the portal just wide enough that the ponies could see through. All of them gasped as they saw into the strange world for the first time.
"You are going on vacation, but not on Equestria," Celestia laughed, "I've found this little gateway just today. It leads into an entirely different world. Inhabited by these creatures."
She pulled her plaything out of a pocket in her dress. As much as she would have loved to have kept it stuffed in her underwear, she didn't want to be so crass when she had visitors. Besides, it wouldn't be that long until she was alone with it again...

"What is this?" Fluttershy suddenly flew forward, snatching the human out of Celestia's hand. Her usual shyness was forgotten as soon as she saw the unusual animal. Curious, she raised it to her face, trying to get a better look.
Of course, she didn't know that Celestia had obliterated its free will, so she simply thought it to be a very well trained animal when it got close to her nose and rubbed itself against it.
"This is a human, my dear," the princess answered, trying to hide her smile as she saw that her pet had become completely tame, even to other ponies. "This world is called 'Earth', and humans live there. Not all of them are like this one, tough. Some of them might be afraid of you, at first. This is another reason why I'm sending you there - to study these humans. But don't worry," she said, turning her gaze to Twilight, "this is not an assignment. The most important thing is for you to have fun, and nothing else."

Twilight and her friends looked at each other, excitement beginning to build. Of course, Fluttershy wanted nothing more than to throw herself through the portal right then and there. After all, a whole species of cute animals lay on the other side. Twilight was the same, though she was more interested in visiting another world.
Like both of them, each of her friends had there own ideas. Applejack and Rainbow Dash were already hectically discussing swimming through the ocean. If the whole planet was smaller, they would be titans and could probably try to race each other around the equator. Pinkie was shivering, thinking of all the tasty alien treats she could find there. And Rarity... she ogled the human in Fluttershy's grasp. Then, she turned to Celestia.

"Tell me, princess, how sentient are these humans? It seems like they can be trained?"
"Oh yes, Rarity. They are very receptive to magic and you can easily communicate to them, if you want."
"Perfect. I wanted to visit the spa anyway. Maybe I can get them to pamper me, instead."
"Rarity!" Fluttershy yelped, "You can't make them do that! They are so..."
"Don't worry, Fluttershy," the princess said, a devious smirk on her face, "You can do whatever you want with the humans. As I said, they are very agreeable."
"If... if you say so, princess..." Fluttershy mumbled.

"Well then, what are we waiting for?" Rainbow shouted, itching to get going, "Everyone get dressed and then we can visit this human world!"

The friends nodded at each other, and after only a few minutes, each of them arrived back in Celestia's bedroom with a fitting outfit of what they wanted to do.

Fluttershy had opted for her normal clothes, since she was planning to spend most of her time with the humans. She was wearing a simple knee-length skirt, which flared widely against her hips, a creme colored sweater, and red sneakers.
Twilight was much the same, sporting her usual white tank top, as well as form fitting, dark red leggins. She opted to go barefoot, though, and was clutching a small package filled with all kinds of arcane equipment to study this new world.

Rainbow Dash and Applejack were both clad in bikinis and nothing else, except for Applejack's hat, of course. Rainbow's bikini was black and skimpy, almost scandalous. A cloud with a rainbow colored lightning bolt shooting out of it was emblazoned on the back of her bottoms. Applejack's bikini was much more modest, cupping her full breasts in a generous, red top, while her shorts emphasized her muscle laden figure.

Pinkie had chosen a blue one piece swim suit, which barely contained her curves. Though her first priority was to sample the local sweets, she would never say no to a bath!
Rarity was clothed in an elegant, orange sun dress, a wide brimmed hat, and sandals, leaving her toes bare.

"Well then," Celestia giggled, taking her pet back from Fluttershy, "I guess you are all ready. Simply walk through the portal and enjoy your vacation!"

The six ponies all shouted their appreciation... and finally, one by one, they walked towards the portal. Their arrival in the human world was imminent...

15421113321234321211213211112112233313.content:
The shimmering portal that had once showed the vista of a city changed to that of a bright and beautiful sandy beach stretching along an alien coastline.
Over a couple of thousand people were gathered at the beach playing in the sand, laying in beach chairs or out on carefully rolled out towels or mats soaking in the sun, gleefully swimming in the water, or searching the coastline for washed-up seashells.

They were blissfully unaware of any other worlds out there, or the chaos that was about to arrive.

In a fraction of an instant, before the people on the beach had any time to react, a huge boom reverberated across the water, causing many of them to suddenly sit up and pay attention to an area over the water. Immediately after that, a loud, low-frequency buzzing filled the air, pulsing to a steady beat like the earth itself had decided to throw a rave party.

Fearing an earthquake or some kind of tsunami, most of the beachgoers hightailed it out of there and began running away from the disturbance and toward safety, either toward their cars or toward one of the buildings lying out on the fringe.

Some brave (or stupid) souls chose to stay behind and began documenting the event with their phones or recording equipment they had on hand. Something big was about to go down and they wanted the whole world to see it.
They didn't have long to wait, as the air around a certain stretch of the ocean began to ripple and shimmer while also glowing in a bluish tint that slowly began to build in intensity.

For a time, nothing seemed to happen. Other than the Then massive purple foot passed through the portal, impacting the ground and sending a huge plume of sand high up into the air. When its sister emerged, all those who were
watching nearby in a mile radius (even those inside the relatively safe buildings) went into full-blown panic mode and began moving far inland, trying to get as far away from the beach as they could.

They didn't see the owner of the sneakers fully emerge, nor did they see the five other multicolored titans emerge behind her, or they might've ended up being scared out of their wits.

The purple creature turned on her heel to face her bikini-clad friends, who were by themselves massively deforming the sandy landscape on which they stood, and began to address them.

"Huh, I really thought there would be more humans around here to greet us, I guess I was mistaken."

"Well, from the looks of things our size must be too intimidating for them. Princess Celestia did say that some of them might be afraid of us, at first. If we give them some time they might be more willing to come to us," said Fluttershy, who's hair was steady flapping in the strong salty wind..

Rarity meanwhile clasped her hands together and gave Twilight a pointed look. "I think that if we were to split up and fan out, then we would be sure to find some who are hiding nearby. I for one would hate for the poor darlings to see us as some kind of monsters. Perhaps there's some way we can convince them that we're friends?"

Twilight beamed at her friend's suggestions and quickly nodded. "Agreed, although I'm not so sure if we should split up, just yet. This world is strange and new, and we have no idea what it holds in store for us. It might be a good idea to err to the side of caution, just in case."

Fluttershy and Rarity
Applejack and Rainbow Dash
More combinations

154211133212343212112132111121122333132.title: The group decides to pair off and go exploring

choices: That she wanted to play a prank on the humans!
That she wanted to introduce herself to the humans!
She tells AJ about her urges - which Applejack shares!
1542111332123432121121321111211223331323.content:
"Well, I don't know about you guys," Rainbow yelled. She had split off from the group and had waded into the sea until her ankles were completely underwater. A massive grin spread over her face as she turned to her friends, especially eyeing up Applejack.
"But I could go for a swim. I'll look for humans later, but for now, I just want to enjoy being at the beach! It's been like, what, two years since the last time? And this is an alien beach on top of that! That's so awesome."

"Well, hope ya' don't mind me comin' along," Applejack smiled, walking towards her friend, every step of hers blowing up the sandy beach beneath her.
"Of course not!" Rainbow Dash answered. She looked at Applejack's towering form and a smirk crossed her lips. "I was actually thinking... what about a race?"
"Would never say no to that, Rainbow," Applejack laughed. She turned back to the others. "We'll have our fun here, alright?"

"Alright!" Twilight said. She intended to make contact and study the humans. Pinkie would be tagging along, because... she wanted to? Or maybe she thought she would have the best chance of getting her hands on the local cuisine that way. Meanwhile, Fluttershy and Rarity had paired up, both intending to see the sights and get a closer look at the human cities. Fluttershy, of course, only wanted the best and hoped to make some cute, new friends!
"We'll be meeting back here when it gets dark, okay? I'll try to get us a place to sleep, but if I can't, we'll just go back to Equestria. Princess Celestia taught me the spell to open the portal."

Everyone nodded, and soon, only Rainbow Dash and Applejack were left on the beach, both stretching their arms and legs in preparation for their race. Rainbow smirked at her friend.
"You ready?"
"As much as I could be. No wings?"
"Really?"
"Come on. Lets keep it fair. Its a swimming race, after all."
"Fine."
"Alright. Let me just..." Applejack laid her trusty hat onto the ground. Normally she wouldn't just leave it like that, but she doubted that anything on this planet would be able to steal it. And she barely felt a breeze. It would definitely still be there for her once she got back.
"Now," Rainbow said, getting ready to jump into the sea, "Three. Two. One."

As if they were copies of each other, both Rainbow Dash and Applejack leaped forwards at the exact same time, both plunging themselves into the comfortably cool water. They didn't even notice that they unleashed tsunami like waves with their weight, which rolled over the coast. Thankfully, the humans had evacuated as soon as they saw the six titans. Still, it had only taken a single movement of them to destroy multiple villages simply by jumping into the water.
Of course, property damage on a minuscule scale was the last thing on their minds right now. There was a race to win! But as was so often the case, it seemed to be coming to a draw. Neither of them managed to overtake the other and they simply plowed through the water alongside each other, sending wave after wave across the ocean. Minutes passed and they didn't slow down. None of them wanted to concede, and the water felt nice, anyway.

But then, as they swam at unimaginable speeds through the water, Applejack spied something on the water. A boat! Or rather, a ship. A large, white one, looking almost like a swimming house. A cruise ship. The orange mare slowed down and raised her head above water to get a better look. The ship was filled to the brim with humans, all of them looking terrified at the blue blur swimming through the ocean, heading directly towards them. Rainbow Dash, completely absorbed in the competition, hadn't noticed the ship.
Applejack quickly threw herself forward. She knew that she had only a moment to avert disaster. Drawing on all her strength, she pushed herself closer to Rainbow until her tail was in reach. She raised her hand and...

"Ouch! Hey, no fair!" Furious, Rainbow rose out of the water after Applejack had yanked her tail, bringing her to a complete stop. Applejack could only let out a relived sigh. Even now, the cruise ship just feet away from Rainbow, the blue mare hadn't noticed it. She would have just plowed through if she had kept swimming. Now, the humans were only showered by the spray of her emerging from the water.
"What was that for!" Rainbow demanded. She wasn't even mad that Applejack had yanked her tail, only that the competition had been ended prematurely.
"To stop you from hitting that," Applejack simply pointed to the ship.
"Hitting what... Oh!" Finally, Rainbow saw the ship as well, and her face turned apologetic. "I was so absorbed into swimming that I didn't notice that, huh?"
"Well, that's what I'm here for," Applejack slapped her friend on the shoulder, "Now, do you want to keep going?"

Rainbow thought for a moment. Then, she turned her gaze to the ship once more, leaning closer. The humans on board were terrified, but there was nowhere they could go to flee the two titans. Something tingled within Rainbow, a low and unfamiliar feeling. Seeing these helpless creatures, she couldn't help but feel powerful. She knew that there was nothing that could stop her from doing whatever she wanted...
Quickly, though, she shook those thoughts out of her mind and turned to Applejack again.
"Why don't we say hello? I think I need to apologize, anyway. And weren't we supposed to be making contact?"
"How very thoughtful of you," Applejack's eyes narrowed, "Its not like you to let a competition go. You aren't planning anything, are ya'?"
"Maybe I am," Rainbow grinned.
"Well, whatever it is, don't hurt them, alright?"
"Sure, sure. Now, come closer... I want to tell you something..."

Applejack leaned forward and Rainbow Dash started to whisper into her ear. And she told her...

1542111332123432121121321111211223331332.content:
While everyone else left to tend to their own devices, Rarity made haste along the beach crunching sand underfoot and leaving huge footprints as she went seeking one thing and one thing only.
Actually, maybe two things, maybe more. She of course wanted to meet all the darling little humans acquire more knowledge about a strange new world, that much was true.
However, a more pressing matter had come up, one that in Rarity's mind came before all else in her hierarchy of needs (besides her friends' safety and well-being of course) and that was looking her best all the time, and glowing with inner beauty, and being pampered and fawned over.

Rarity knew that there had to be a human city somewhere near the coastline if her intuition was correct, and everypony know that where there's a city, there's a spa.

Deep shiatsu massages, essential oils and fragrances, seaweed wraps, exfoliating mud baths, manis and pedis galore, she wanted to be pampered and treated like a queen.

With proper monetary compensation of course. She wasn't some kind of nasty slave driver after all like some other ponies in her position might be, although the more she thought about it, those little nimble hands could be used for other purposes as well, especially in the workshop... or other places... like the bedroom...

Rarity shook off that last thought and scolded herself, You should be using your wonderful brain to find the humans and stop thinking with your lady parts. Priorities, dear.

Just as expected. after taking long strides down the beach Rarity could see large buildings coming up the horizon that was unmistakable for beach hotels and resorts and she could feel herself getting a little giddy.
This is what Pinkie must feel like when she's about to make new friends Rarity thought.

Rarity crashed up the beach in a full sprint, eager to get near civilization as soon as possible and not really taking much attention for what might be underfoot.
She unwittingly crushed a hotdog stand, which, thankfully was abandoned by its owner after the initial call for evacuations came in.

It only took three great strides for Rarity to close the gap and she was there, ready for her spa treatment.
There was only one problem. There was not a single soul in sight and aside from the crashing of surf in the distance, the air was eerily still and silent.

"Ok, this is starting to creep me out. Where is everyone?" Rarity muttered, and then called out, "Uh. hello, is anyone in there? I'm a customer that wishes to be served."

There was no response, at least, not an intentional one. Rarity thought she saw movement flicker near the entrance of one the hotels, and she pretty something just moved behind one of the windows.

Rarity sighed and crooked an arm under chin, and began thinking. She was fairly certain that the humans had gone into hiding, and that they were hiding because they were afraid of her.
How was she going to convince them that she wasn't a monster and didn't mean any harm?
She needed to hit upon some kind of solution and quickly.

1542111332123432121121321111211223331333.content:
Fluttershy was last to break off from the group. No matter how exciting it was to visit a new world, she still felt anxious when alone. Normally, she had the company of her pets, but for now, she was all by herself on a strange, alien planet. Who knew what dangers lurked here? What would she do if something attacked her? Worst of all, were there dragons in this world? Maybe she should just return...

With a firm shake of her head, Fluttershy broke out of her anxiety. If there would be anything dangerous here, the princess would have told her. She trusted Celestia completely. And the only thing she mentioned were those humans.
A delighted smile spread over Fluttershy's face. Yes, that was right! This world was the home of these incredible cute creatures. Why did she worry about what would happen to her? Right now, she seemed to be the biggest thing around, and a whole world of new animals to discover!

Suddenly, you felt light. Not light headed, just... your entire body felt as if it was floating through a comforting void. You couldn't feel the weight of the enormous breasts on you anymore. Neither could you feel the ache in your muscles. In fact, you felt good! As if you had never exhausted yourself.

You opened your eyes and gasped as you saw the bright stars shining above you. The sky was a perfect night blue, lit by millions of little lights. It looked like one of those pictures, taken on a Caribbean isle where no one lived. You could see constellations you'd never seen before and so much more. Even what looked to be a nebula was visible to your naked eye!

As you sat there in awe, you didn't notice the sky slowly shifting, the stars drifting to the side. Until they appeared.
Two absolutely massive eyes appeared in front of you as the sky fell away to reveal the gigantic face of another of those massive creatures.
Terror filled you as you saw the gargantuan pupils focus on you. Whatever you had imagined it to feel like for the giantess to find you, you couldn't have imagined the primal fear of being in front of a creature this powerful. You curled up, hoping against hope that if you just made yourself small enough, the giantess would lose interest in you.

Of course, that didn't happen, and dread rose in your stomach as you could feel the massive being get closer to you. A wave of air washed over you as the creature exhaled.

"And how did you get here...?"

Your entire body froze as you heard the angelic voice ring out. It wasn't as deafening as you had believed it. In fact, it was downright pleasant. Beautiful, even! Slowly, you turned, seeing that the giantess was looking at you with a mix of curiosity and pity.

"What a strange creature. I wonder if Celestia would know about it," the giantess mumbled, more to herself than to you.

Finally, you broke out of your stupor. What were you doing? This being was obviously intelligent, not to mention caring! This was your opportunity to make contact!

But should you? By the way she was looking at you, she didn't seem to think you could understand her. How would she react if she found out you were, in fact, intelligent? Old horror stories, of government scientists dissecting a strange new thing, came unbidden to your mind. Maybe it was best to just play the fool for now...

154211133221.content:
"U-um, hello there," you squeaked, not sure at all of yourself and not wanting to provoke her ire.
The giant's ears twitched at the noise, her eyebrows jumped in mild surprise and then a (relatively) small smile graced the mountain-sized mare's lips.
"Ah, so he can speak. Funny, I was beginning to wonder if I was dealing with a little bug. Since you're feeling talkative, would you mind telling me your name, little one?" Her voice contained a teasing lilt, with just a little bit of mirth behind it.
Her face looming right next to you was unnerving, and you could feel the moist flow of air pushing your hair back every time she exhaled. Her breath was sweet and smelled strongly of hay or alfalfa. Given that she's a horse woman that's likely what she had for dinner that night.
Still, the warmth in her tone was unmistakable, and it made you relax your shoulders a little bit.
You took in a deep breath. gulped down your fears and expelled a puff of air from your nose. before continuing. "Y-yes, Miss Giant Horse Lady ma'am, my name's Anon and I'm a human being. I uh... I'm... not really from around here."
She folded her hand beneath her chin in deep thought. "That much is obvious. I have never seen a creature of you like before, not in my many years of living. Since we're in the midst of introducing ourselves, you may call me Luna. I am the mistress of the night, the realm of dreams is my dominion over which I have absolute control, and I wield that power to ensure that my subjects receive a restful night's sleep. You can imagine my surprise and shock when I sensed something new entering from the Aether. Tell me, Anon, from what land do you hail?"
That was going to be a difficult question to answer. Would she believe that a mystical portal appeared in your living room and you threw yourself in on a whim?
Well... Honesty was the best policy, and you had a feeling that she would be able to tell if you're lying. "Well... Miss Luna... would you believe that I come from another world? There was a portal that suddenly appeared in my room and things led to another I came forth into it and landed someone sleeping." You gulped a bit nervous but remained even, You continue. "I went to her head to wake her up and get her attention but was futile and..." The last part was a bit hard, it would involve the fact you climbed into her exposed cleavage and currently asleep nestled in it.
"I...see.," she said slowly while nodding her head, "The initial jump was intentional, where you ended up was where the misstep occurred. I thank you for your honesty on the matter."(edited)
You perked up on this "Excuse me but... what are you talking about?" Was your entrance to this dimension, intentional?
"I mean that you chose to come here without knowing where you would land, and thus found yourself in your current predicament," she clarified. "I know not of what created the portal or whether it was natural or not, I was merely making an observation."
"Oh..." You stated, " Well... Ket's just say I'm sort of stuck in a sensitive situation, and in trying to get out I exhausted myself and that's where I fell asleep," you confessed sheepishly.
Luna's normally calm gaze instantly took on an air of deep concern. "Oh my! Are you quite alright? You're not hurt, are you?"
You rapidly shook your head and waved your hands. "Oh, no no no. Believe me, I'm somewhere very soft and very safe. In fact, when I'll probably be able to escape when I get all my strength and energy back. It's just that the location I'm stuck in is a little bit questionable."

Luna took about a minute to respond, carefully poring over everything you'd told her before she said...

1542111332212.content:
"Your sister?" you asked.
"Oh yes," the midnight-blue titan answered, "Celestia will certainly want to know that an outsider has entered our realm. Do not worry, little human, she is by far wiser than even I. If there is one person on Equus, that would know how to find a way home for you, it would be her."

You chewed over what Luna had just said. A way home? Already? Not that your exploration had gone swimmingly so far, but you somehow doubted that another extra planar portal would manifest itself in your room anytime soon. This was probably your one and only chance to explore this world.
But having some of these giantesses looking out for you would certainly make this expedition into the unknown a lot easier. Especially Luna. Even though you had only seen one other member of her species so far, she had an extraordinary sense of power about her. 'Mistress of the night' she had called herself.
You gulped. Had you by accident encountered the royalty of this strange land? The way she spoke of this 'Celestia' only made you more nervous. If even Luna, a being powerful enough to rule over the land of dreams, would adhere to this person, you could only imagine how powerful she must be.

You shook your head. What were you worried about? Luna had been nothing but kind, so far, and having her and her sister on your side would surely only help you. Besides, you had to return to your own life at some point, didn't you? It would be a shame to want to return, only to find that there was no way back...

Looking back up at Luna, you realized she seemed just as deep in thought as you. Her eyes were slightly unfocused, as if she was staring at something behind you. Worried, you cleared your throat. "Uhm... Luna?"
The giantess broke out of her trance as soon as you spoke, returning her gaze towards you.
"Oh, my apologies, human! I was simply telling my sister of your arrival. She is looking forward to meeting you!"
"Great..." you mumbled. Of course, you were thankful, but your nervousness still lingered. The prospect of meeting with this Celestia outside of the safety of this dreamland loomed ominously over you.
"But... how will I find her? I don't really know where I am... at least not exactly. And I doubt the things I do know would be helpful..." you trailed off. You thought better than to tell Luna the fact that you were sleeping in someone's cleavage. There was no need for anyone to know that.
"Oh, its very simple!" Luna said, smiling down at you, "You said that you had appeared near someone?"
"Yes," you answered, glad that she apparently hadn't noticed your embarrassment, "There is another one of your kind sleeping nearby."
"Perfect! Then all you need to do, is to get their attention. Do not be afraid, Equestria's inhabitants are perfectly kind. You do not have to fear danger from them. Simply tell the pony you have encountered that you need to reach Canterlot and talk with the princesses! They will certainly bring you to us."
"Alright..." Of course, you would first have to explain your position to the 'pony' in who's cleavage you were currently encased. Even though Luna seemed confident that you had nothing to fear, you couldn't imagine that this giantess would simply let you get away with that. Hopefully you would have the chance to sneak out, once you woke up.

"Now, little human, I sadly must return to my duties," Luna said suddenly, "But I'm glad that we have met! I am looking forward to meeting you in the waking world, soon!" With that, she pushed herself upwards, rising to her full height above you. You stumbled backwards. You had thought that you had gotten used to her massive height during your conversation, but... it still took you off guard.
"Y-Yeah," you stammered, "Hopefully I'll meet you and Celestia soon... if I can reach the pony..."
"Very well. If you wish, I can return to you tomorrow night - and you can tell me if you have any difficulties."

You nodded. Luna gave you one last smile. Then, she opened her wings and with a mighty flap, she shot into the night sky above you. The ripple of the air she so casually displaced sent you flying, tumbling into... nothing. The world you had been in faded and you could feel yourself fall back into a restful sleep as you were once again submerged into the familiar darkness...
